description: 2 train/test blocks, 8 words each; 1 word and 4 objects per trial; 6 repetitions per word; 2 meanings per word: 1 present whenever the there; one in only half the case; other objects appeared only once with any other word (17%). Manipulated the order in which trials including and excluding the 50% referent were presented within four levels (within participants): Firstly, the 50%-present (P) and 50%-absent (A) trials could be either blocked (AAAPPP and PPPAAA) or not blocked (APAPAP and PAPAPA); secondly, the first encounter of a noun could be either an A trial (AAAPPP and APAPAP) or a P trial (PPPAAA and PAPAPA)
citation: Koehne, J., Trueswell, J. C, & Gleitman, L. R. (2013). Multiple Proposal Memory in Observational Word Learning. Proceedings of the Annual Meeting of the Cognitive Science Society, 35.
